Docs.title: From marketplace to MCP in one clear workflow
Docs.description: Connect an agent client, manage skills and collections, understand credits and requestId, and troubleshoot MCP calls without exposing your key.
Docs.startDescription: Your dashboard is the control plane; your MCP-compatible client is where skills are used.

account.title: Create your account
account.description: Sign up to receive 50 trial credits and open the OceanSkill dashboard.

key.title: Create an MCP key
key.description: Create a dedicated key in Dashboard → MCP keys. Copy it immediately because the full key is shown once.

client.title: Configure your client
client.description: Add the Supabase Edge Function endpoint and send the key in the Authorization header.

verify.title: Verify the connection
verify.description: Ask your agent to list OceanSkill tools, search the catalog, or show your available skills.

Docs.connectTitle: Add the MCP server to your client
Docs.connectDescription: Use this JSON shape in Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, or another MCP client. Replace both placeholders with your project endpoint and MCP key.
Docs.configWarning: Keep keys local. Never commit an nsk_ key, paste it into frontend code, or share it in screenshots.

clientNotes.url: serverUrl points to the deployed Supabase Edge Function: /functions/v1/mcp.
clientNotes.key: Authorization uses Bearer followed by the nsk_ key created in your dashboard.
clientNotes.reload: After saving, restart or reload the MCP client so it discovers the latest tools.

Docs.workflowDescription: Public pages help you evaluate skills. Your library and collections decide what your agent can use.

discover.title: Discover
discover.description: Browse categories, authors, compatibility, versions, tags, and public descriptions.

enable.title: Enable
enable.description: Add a skill or collection to your library, then enable only the skills you want exposed to your agent.

invoke.title: Invoke
invoke.description: Ask the agent to use a named skill. OceanSkill returns the scanned current version through authenticated MCP.

Docs.publishingTitle: Publish private skills and add slots when needed
Docs.publishingDescription: Every account includes 5 private-skill slots. The dashboard identifies the signed-in user as the author, while verified content and hashes remain protected behind Storage and MCP.

upload.title: Upload a skill package
upload.description: Open Dashboard → My skills → Uploaded skills, enter metadata, then provide SKILL.md or a reviewed ZIP/RAR package.

limit.title: Use 5 included slots
limit.description: Platform skills added to your library do not count. Only private skills you publish consume these slots.

payment.title: 5,000 VND per extra slot
payment.description: When the allowance is full, Billing opens at 5,000 VND. Choose a larger multiple to buy several slots in one payment.

Docs.collectionsDescription: Discover platform collections in the skill marketplace. They appear in the dashboard only after you add them, where three tabs separate all, platform, and user-owned collections.

browse.title: Browse in the marketplace
browse.description: Open a platform collection card to review its description and every included skill before adding it.

add.title: Add it explicitly
add.description: Choose Add to my collections. Until then, the platform collection remains outside your dashboard.

manage.title: Use three dashboard tabs
manage.description: Switch between All, Platform, and Mine. Platform collections are read-only; owned collections can be created, edited, and deleted.

Docs.requestDescription: requestId is a per-call idempotency key for get_skill_md and get_skill_reference. Its exact-replay window lasts 10 minutes, and most MCP clients can let OceanSkill generate it automatically.

new.title: New work, new ID
new.description: Use a new requestId for a new skill-content attempt. It may contain 1–120 characters.

retry.title: Retry within 10 minutes
retry.description: Reuse the same requestId only for the same tool, skill, current version, and reference within 10 minutes. OceanSkill marks it replayed and charges 0 extra credits.

failed.title: A failed attempt needs a new ID
failed.description: Failed calls release their reservation and explain whether retry is allowed. Start the next attempt with a new requestId.

conflict.title: Never reuse it across scopes
conflict.description: Using one requestId for another skill, version, or tool returns REQUEST_ID_CONFLICT instead of charging ambiguously.

Docs.creditDescription: Each successful new get_skill_md or get_skill_reference call costs 1 credit. OceanSkill reserves one credit before reading, finalizes the debit only after success, and releases the reservation after failure. Search, listing, collection, toggle, and usage-summary tools remain free.
Docs.toolsDescription: These are the tools exposed by the current OceanSkill MCP server.

list.name: list_purchased_skills
list.description: List enabled public and owned private skills available to the account.

search.name: search_skills
search.description: Search available metadata without exposing protected SKILL.md content.

content.name: get_skill_md
content.description: Download the scanned current SKILL.md directly from private Storage and return available reference keys; accepts an optional requestId.

reference.name: get_skill_reference
reference.description: Return one mapped file from the current skill version. Text uses UTF-8 and binary content uses base64.

collections.name: list_collections
collections.description: List public collections and collections owned by the authenticated user.

addCollection.name: add_collection_to_library
addCollection.description: Add an accessible collection to the user library and enable its skills.

createCollection.name: create_skill_collection
createCollection.description: Create a private collection with a name, collection key, and ordered skill list.

updateCollection.name: update_skill_collection
updateCollection.description: Rename, describe, re-key, or replace the skills in a user-owned collection.

deleteCollection.name: delete_skill_collection
deleteCollection.description: Delete a user-owned collection. Platform collections cannot be deleted.

executeCollection.name: execute_skill_collection
executeCollection.description: Fetch SKILL.md sequentially for up to 10 skills in an owned collection; each successful fetch costs 1 credit.

toggle.name: toggle_skill
toggle.description: Enable or disable one library skill without fetching paid content.

usage.name: get_usage_summary
usage.description: Return the current credit balance and month-to-date MCP usage.

auth.description: Confirm the Authorization header uses Bearer and an active nsk_ key from the same account.

credits.description: Open Billing to top up. The response is clear and no content credit is charged.

access.description: Add or enable the skill in your library and confirm that its current version is active.

conflict.description: Follow the returned retryable flag. Use a new requestId after failure or version change.

server.description: The response includes a stable errorCode and may include requestId. Keep those values when reporting the issue; do not include your key.

Docs.ctaDescription: Create an MCP key, add your first collection, and check usage from the dashboard.
